skill.name: add-endpoint
skill.description: Guides developers through adding HTTP endpoints to the Catalyst-Relay Hono server with route files, validation, wiring, and documentation.
skill.summary: Add Catalyst-Relay HTTP endpoints with consistent schemas, handlers, route registration, responses, and docs.
skill.author: Artisan-Edge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, relative import examples, and documentation text as command execution, path traversal, weak crypto, and reconnaissance. Review found only instructional TypeScript examples and endpoint documentation guidance, with no executable skill code, network access, secret handling, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Documentation
low_findings.description: The reported command, traversal, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns occur inside Markdown examples and checklist text. The skill contains no executable script, shell invocation, network call, environment access, or prompt-injection instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is an instructional SKILL.md with code snippets and paths only. No evidence found of executable behavior or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Adding endpoints can become inconsistent when route files, validation, handlers, and docs follow different patterns. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear Catalyst-Relay checklist for consistent HTTP endpoint work.
actual_capabilities: Defines the route file pattern under src/server/routes/{category}/{endpoint}.ts.
Shows colocated Zod request schemas, response types, and handler factories.
Explains how to register Hono routes in src/server/routes/index.ts.
Specifies the standard success and error response envelope.
Lists endpoint documentation requirements for docs/endpoints/{category}.md.
Maps HTTP endpoints to ADTClient library methods when needed.
limitations: It is specific to the Catalyst-Relay server structure.
It does not implement endpoint business logic for the developer.
It does not define authentication or authorization policy.
It does not run tests or type checks by itself.

use_cases.title: Add a New API Route
use_cases.description: Create a route file with validation, response typing, and a Hono handler that matches Catalyst-Relay conventions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Endpoint Documentation
use_cases.description: Produce endpoint docs with request tables, response tables, examples, errors, use cases, and library usage notes.

use_cases.title: Expose Core Features Through HTTP
use_cases.description: Connect core functionality to an HTTP endpoint and add a matching ADTClient method when library mode needs it.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-endpoint skill to add a simple Catalyst-Relay endpoint for [feature]. Include the route file, request validation, response type, and route registration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation and Responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-endpoint skill to design an endpoint for [operation]. Follow the standard Zod schema pattern and success or error response envelope.

prompt_templates.title: Document an Endpoint
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-endpoint skill to create documentation for [endpoint]. Include request details, body fields, response fields, errors, use cases, and library usage.

prompt_templates.title: Add HTTP and Client Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the add-endpoint skill to add [endpoint] and map it to an ADTClient method. Ensure route wiring, validation, response format, docs, and typecheck guidance are covered.

output_examples.input: Add a login endpoint to Catalyst-Relay.
output: A route file plan for the auth category.
A request validation and response typing checklist.
A route registration step for the Hono app.
Documentation items for the auth endpoint page.
A documentation outline with request and response tables.
A use case list for package discovery.
A library usage note for the matching client method.

best_practices: Keep each endpoint in its own route file with colocated schema, types, and handler.
Use the standard response envelope for both success and error cases.
Update route registration, documentation, and ADTClient mappings in the same change when applicable.
anti_patterns: Do not add handlers without Zod request validation.
Do not return ad hoc response shapes that bypass the success and error envelope.
Do not leave endpoint documentation or client mappings for a later unrelated change.

faq.question: What project is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the Catalyst-Relay server and its Hono route structure.

faq.question: Does this skill create production-ready business logic?
faq.answer: No. It defines the endpoint structure, validation, response format, wiring, and documentation expectations.

faq.question: Which validation library does the pattern use?
faq.answer: The examples use Zod request schemas colocated with each endpoint.

faq.question: Where should endpoint documentation go?
faq.answer: The skill directs documentation to docs/endpoints/{category}.md.

faq.question: When should ADTClient be updated?
faq.answer: Update ADTClient when the HTTP endpoint wraps core functionality that should also be available in library mode.

faq.question: Does the skill run the type checker?
faq.answer: No. It tells the developer to run the project typecheck after adding the endpoint.
